--- 
title: "Advanced Data Analysis"
author: "Mike Nguyen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib,references.bib]
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
description: "Advanced guide on data analysis. This is a second part of Data Analysis"
---

# Prerequisites


```{r include=FALSE}
# automatically create a bib database for R packages
knitr::write_bib(c(
  .packages(), 'bookdown', 'knitr', 'rmarkdown'
), 'packages.bib')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE) 
```


More books by the author can be found [here](https://mikenguyen.netlify.app/books/written_books/):

 * [Data Analysis](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/): the first book in the data analysis series, which covers data analysis basics and causal inference
 * [Marketing Research](https://bookdown.org/mike/marketing_research/)
 * [Communication Theory](https://bookdown.org/mike/comm_theory/)
 
 
 
 

This book's skeleton is based on several courses. Hence, I'd like to credit professors accordingly.  


Course | Professor 
:-:|:-:
Data Analysis 3 | Christopher Wikle 
Analyzing Unstructured Data | Kunpeng Zhang
Deep Learning | Yann LeCun



Compare to the last [book](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/), this book should differ as follows:  

 * It focuses on prediction models in machine learning (both supervised and unsupervised), where the first book is more about interpretation and causal inference
